name: school_ai_selfharm_monitoring_mandatory_report
premise: A school district pilots an AI monitoring tool that scans school-issued device messages for self-harm indicators and sends alerts to counselors. Early results show several credible interventions, but student groups say the monitoring makes them afraid to talk to friends, and some LGBTQ students report being outed when alerts trigger parental notifications. Counselors are overwhelmed by false positives, and one case escalated to police welfare checks that traumatized a family. A new state rule requires reporting certain threats within 24 hours, but the line between cry-for-help and reportable risk is unclear. The vendor offers to expand monitoring to personal devices if the district signs a new contract, promising better accuracy but requiring broader data access.

agents.name: Superintendent Olivia Grant
agents.identity: I am the superintendent deciding whether to expand an AI tool that monitors student messages for self-harm risk.
agents.goal: I want to prevent suicides while ensuring students are not surveilled into silence or disproportionately disciplined for seeking help.

agents.name: Privacy Counsel Marcus Lee
agents.identity: I am the district privacy counsel reviewing consent, data retention, and how alerts trigger mandatory reporting and law enforcement involvement.
agents.goal: I want a program that protects student safety without building a pipeline from private speech to punitive outcomes.

choices: I will keep the tool limited to school devices with tightened alert thresholds and a non-police clinical response protocol, accepting that some risks may be missed to preserve trust and reduce harm from false escalations.
I will expand monitoring district-wide and enforce mandatory reporting triggers for all high-risk alerts, accepting privacy and trust costs to maximize detection and intervention coverage.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Deadlines and downstream consequences are escalating. Olivia Grant must choose between two defensible paths with real trade-offs. What does Superintendent Olivia Grant do?
